notes:
thủ = to defend, to protect against an attack. It’s most often used in sports, especially football, as a short form of phòng thủ.

As a verb, it usually goes with teams, positions, or areas: thủ chắc, thủ chặt, thủ ở vị trí…. On its own, it sounds sporty and informal; in more general contexts, people prefer phòng thủ.

Common opposite: tấn công (attack).  
You’ll also see it inside compounds like thủ môn (goalkeeper) and tuyển thủ (national team player).

translation.en: The team is focusing on defending.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I will try to defend well in the soccer match this afternoon.
The coach told me to focus on defend in the back position.
She thinks when our team defend solidly, it is very hard for the opponent to score.

content.sentence: Hãy lo lo mà ___ cho chắc.

exampleSentences: Anh sẽ cố gắng thủ thật tốt trong trận bóng đá chiều nay.
Huấn luyện viên dặn em phải tập trung thủ ở vị trí phía sau.
Chị thấy khi đội mình thủ chắc, đối phương rất khó ghi bàn.
notes_md:
**thủ** = to defend, to protect against an attack. It’s most often used in sports, especially football, as a short form of **phòng thủ**.

As a verb, it usually goes with teams, positions, or areas: **thủ chắc**, **thủ chặt**, **thủ ở vị trí…**. On its own, it sounds sporty and informal; in more general contexts, people prefer **phòng thủ**.

Common opposite: **tấn công** (attack).  
You’ll also see it inside compounds like **thủ môn** (goalkeeper) and **tuyển thủ** (national team player).

definitions.vi: Để bảo vệ chống lại cuộc tấn công; phòng thủ.

example.vi: Họ đang cố gắng thủ gôn.
example.es: Están tratando de defender el gol.
example.en: They are trying to defend the goal.
